cards.title: Billing Remains Stubbed And Approval-Gated
cards.summary: Billing is still in a safe setup posture. Plan visibility and entitlement logic can work, but live money actions remain intentionally blocked until external billing secrets and webhook configuration are ready.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Configure the external billing provider only when you are ready for real money flow and webhook verification.

cards.title: Brand Exposure Leakage
cards.summary: A brand exposure leak means an internal/operator name escaped into a customer-facing surface. Internal naming can remain intact, but public surfaces must stay Onyx-only.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Correct the public wording and rerun brand exposure isolation.

cards.title: KeepAlive Escalated Instead Of Retrying Blindly
cards.summary: KeepAlive saw issues it could explain, but it refused to keep retrying because the next action was not low-risk or would have created a restart storm. That is correct governed behavior, not a missed heal.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Review the escalation queue and authorize only the specific recovery that matches the evidence.

cards.title: Mirror Or Checkpoint Incomplete
cards.summary: The checkpoint mirror did not complete cleanly. That means rollback and off-box continuity evidence may be incomplete, even if the stack itself is still up.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Resolve the access issue, then rerun the mirror flow so the checkpoint can return to ok=true and phase=done.

cards.title: Pending Reboot And Caution Host Posture
cards.summary: The host is asking for a reboot. Mason can keep observing and throttling, but it should not reboot the machine automatically because that would interrupt the running stack and may affect active work.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Schedule a controlled Windows reboot when it will not interrupt current work.

cards.title: Promotion Blocked By Regression Guard
cards.summary: Promotion is blocked because the regression guard does not yet have enough evidence to say the current platform is safely better than the comparison baseline. That is a guardrail, not an accidental slowdown.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Review the seeded baseline, accept a trusted baseline when justified, and clear the remaining warning regressions before promotion.

cards.title: Runtime Singleton Or PID Truth Drift
cards.summary: Runtime truth drift means the platform has conflicting answers about which process actually owns a required service. Mason should treat current live listener ownership as authoritative and avoid trusting stale launcher PID leftovers.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Review the canonical live owner if singleton ownership cannot be established safely.

cards.title: Security Or Tenant-Safety Watch Posture
cards.summary: Security and tenant-safety are in a watch posture. That means the platform still has known governance or isolation work to finish, even if the stack is otherwise running.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Review the tenant safety report and close the isolation warnings intentionally.

cards.title: Self-Improvement Remains Review-Gated
cards.summary: The self-improvement governor is doing its job: it is screening ideas, keeping risky changes approval-gated, and refusing to let weak teacher-backed work flow straight into staging.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Review blocked teacher-worthy items manually and approve only the ones with strong evidence.

cards.title: Service Health Or Missing Listener
cards.summary: A required Mason stack service is missing, unhealthy, or not answering on its loopback contract port. Mason should trust the live probe and listener state first, then use the validator and start artifacts to explain what changed.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Review the failing component log and authorize targeted recovery if the service does not return cleanly.

cards.title: Stale Artifact Versus Live Probe Mismatch
cards.summary: A truth mismatch means an older artifact is describing a healthier or different state than the live machine. Mason should trust the freshest authoritative producer or the live loopback probe, not the prettier-looking stale file.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Review whether the stale producer needs to be rerun or repaired.

cards.title: churn_risk_indicators
cards.summary: Moderate-or-higher churn risk tenants=0; completed onboarding tenants=1.
cards.notes_for_reviewer: Watch tenants with weak adoption or incomplete onboarding for disengagement.
